topics.meaning: Resistors in series are connected end to end, and their total resistance is the sum of the individual resistances.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: When resistors are connected one after another in a single path, they are said to be connected in series. In a series combination, the same current flows through each resistor because there is only one path for the current. The total potential difference across the combination is equal to the sum of the potential differences across the individual resistors. The equivalent or total resistance of resistors in series is equal to the sum of their individual resistances, so adding more resistors in series increases the total resistance. A disadvantage of a series circuit is that if one component fails, the entire circuit is broken and all the components stop working.

topics.meaning: Resistors in parallel are connected across the same two points, and the total resistance is less than the smallest individual resistance.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: When resistors are connected between the same two points, providing separate paths for the current, they are said to be connected in parallel. In a parallel combination, the potential difference across each resistor is the same, while the total current is the sum of the currents through the individual resistors. The reciprocal of the equivalent resistance of resistors in parallel is equal to the sum of the reciprocals of the individual resistances, so the total resistance becomes less than the smallest individual resistance. Parallel circuits have the advantage that each component gets the full voltage and can work independently, so if one fails, the others keep working. For this reason, household electrical appliances are connected in parallel.

topics.meaning: The heating effect of electric current is the production of heat when current flows through a resistor.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: When an electric current flows through a conductor that has resistance, the electrical energy is converted into heat, which is called the heating effect of electric current. This happens because the source of energy must keep expending energy to drive the current against the resistance of the conductor. The amount of heat produced is given by Joule's law of heating, which states that the heat produced is directly proportional to the square of the current, the resistance, and the time for which the current flows, written as H = I x I x R x t. This effect is used in many appliances such as electric irons, toasters, heaters, and electric kettles, where heating elements made of high resistance alloys glow and produce heat. The electric bulb also works on this principle, where the filament becomes hot and glows.

topics.meaning: Electric power is the rate at which electrical energy is consumed or dissipated in a circuit.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Electric power is defined as the rate at which electrical energy is consumed or dissipated in an electric circuit. It is given by the product of the potential difference and the current, written as P = V x I, and can also be expressed as P equals I squared times R or V squared divided by R. The SI unit of electric power is the watt, where one watt is the power consumed when one ampere of current flows at a potential difference of one volt. A larger unit is the kilowatt, equal to one thousand watts. The commercial unit of electrical energy is the kilowatt hour, which is the energy consumed when a power of one kilowatt is used for one hour, and this is what is commonly called one unit of electricity.

topics.meaning: Electrical energy is the total energy consumed by a device, calculated from its power and the time of use.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Electrical energy is the total amount of electrical work done or energy consumed when an appliance operates. It is calculated as the product of the electric power and the time for which the appliance is used, written as energy equals power multiplied by time. While the SI unit of energy is the joule, electrical energy used in homes is measured in kilowatt hours because the joule is a very small unit. One kilowatt hour equals 3.6 million joules. Electricity meters in homes record the electrical energy consumed in kilowatt hours, and the electricity bill is based on the number of units used. Using appliances with higher power ratings or for longer times consumes more electrical energy.

topics.meaning: A magnetic field is the region around a magnet where its force acts, represented by magnetic field lines.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: A magnet has two poles, north and south, and the region around a magnet in which its magnetic force can be detected is called the magnetic field. The magnetic field is a vector quantity, having both magnitude and direction, and its direction at a point is taken as the direction of the force on a north pole placed there. Magnetic field lines are used to represent the field, and they are drawn so that the direction of the field at any point is given by the tangent to the field line at that point. These lines emerge from the north pole and merge at the south pole outside the magnet, and they are crowded where the field is strong and far apart where it is weak. Field lines never intersect each other, because at any point the field can have only one direction.

topics.topic: Magnetic Field due to a Current-carrying Conductor
topics.meaning: A current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field around it, as discovered by Oersted.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The scientist Hans Christian Oersted discovered that an electric current produces a magnetic effect, showing a deep connection between electricity and magnetism. When a current flows through a straight conductor, it produces a magnetic field around it in the form of concentric circles. The magnetic field around a straight current-carrying conductor depends on the strength of the current: a larger current produces a stronger field, and the field becomes weaker as we move farther from the conductor. A compass needle placed near the conductor gets deflected, confirming the presence of the magnetic field. The direction of this magnetic field can be found using the right-hand thumb rule. This discovery is the basis of the study of electromagnetism.

topics.meaning: The right-hand thumb rule gives the direction of the magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The right-hand thumb rule is a simple method to find the direction of the magnetic field produced around a straight current-carrying conductor. According to this rule, if we hold the conductor in our right hand such that the thumb points in the direction of the current, then the way the fingers curl around the conductor gives the direction of the magnetic field lines. This rule helps determine the direction of the concentric circular field lines without complex calculation. It is a convenient and widely used rule in the study of electromagnetism. The same idea helps us understand the field produced by a circular loop and a solenoid as well.

topics.topic: Magnetic Field due to a Current through a Loop and Solenoid
topics.meaning: A current-carrying loop and a solenoid produce magnetic fields, with a solenoid behaving like a bar magnet.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: When current flows through a circular loop, every part of the loop produces a magnetic field in the same direction at the centre, so the field at the centre of the loop is much stronger and appears as straight lines perpendicular to the plane of the loop. The magnetic field produced by a loop can be increased by increasing the number of turns and the current. A solenoid is a coil of many circular turns of insulated wire wound closely in the shape of a cylinder. When current flows through a solenoid, it produces a magnetic field similar to that of a bar magnet, with a north pole at one end and a south pole at the other, and the field inside the solenoid is uniform. A strong magnetic field inside a solenoid can be used to magnetise a piece of magnetic material like soft iron, producing an electromagnet.

topics.topic: Force on a Current-carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field
topics.meaning: A current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field experiences a force whose direction is given by Fleming's left-hand rule.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: When a current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field, it experiences a force, because the magnetic field of the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field. The magnitude of the force is largest when the direction of the current is at right angles to the direction of the magnetic field. The direction of this force is given by Fleming's left-hand rule, which states that if the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger of the left hand are stretched mutually perpendicular, with the forefinger pointing in the direction of the magnetic field and the middle finger in the direction of the current, then the thumb points in the direction of the force on the conductor. This principle is used in many devices, including the electric motor, where the force makes a coil rotate.

topics.meaning: An electric motor is a device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy using the magnetic effect of current.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: An electric motor is a rotating device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy. It works on the principle that a current-carrying coil placed in a magnetic field experiences a force and rotates. The motor consists of a rectangular coil placed between the poles of a magnet, and the two ends of the coil are connected to a split ring called a commutator. When current flows through the coil, the two sides of the coil experience forces in opposite directions due to the magnetic field, which makes the coil rotate. The commutator reverses the direction of current in the coil after every half rotation, so the coil keeps rotating in the same direction continuously. Electric motors are used in many appliances such as fans, mixers, washing machines, and water pumps.

topics.meaning: Electromagnetic induction is the production of an electric current in a coil by a changing magnetic field.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Electromagnetic induction is the phenomenon, discovered by Michael Faraday, in which a changing magnetic field in a coil produces an electric current in it. If a coil of wire is connected to a galvanometer and a magnet is moved towards or away from the coil, the galvanometer shows a deflection, indicating that a current has been set up in the coil. The current produced this way is called an induced current, and it flows only as long as there is a change in the magnetic field linked with the coil. The induced current can also be produced by moving the coil relative to the magnet or by changing the current in a nearby coil. The direction of the induced current is given by Fleming's right-hand rule.

topics.meaning: An electric generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy using electromagnetic induction.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: An electric generator is a device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy, working on the principle of electromagnetic induction. In a generator, a coil is rotated rapidly between the poles of a magnet, and as the coil rotates, the magnetic field linked with it changes continuously, inducing a current in the coil. The two ends of the coil are connected to a device that delivers this current to the external circuit. In an alternating current generator, the current produced changes direction periodically, while in a direct current generator a split-ring commutator is used to obtain current in one direction. Generators are used in power stations to produce electricity on a large scale, supplying power to homes, schools, and industries.

topics.meaning: Domestic electric circuits supply power to homes and use safety devices like fuses and earthing to prevent accidents.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Domestic electric circuits bring electricity into our homes through a main supply, usually consisting of a live wire, a neutral wire, and an earth wire. In India the potential difference between the live and neutral wires is about 220 volts. The circuits in a house are connected in parallel so that each appliance gets the full voltage and can be operated independently. A fuse is an important safety device made of a wire with a low melting point, which melts and breaks the circuit when the current becomes too high, protecting appliances from damage. The earth wire, connected to the metal body of appliances, provides a low resistance path to the ground and protects users from electric shock. A short circuit, caused when the live and neutral wires touch, and overloading can cause dangerous heating, which these safety devices help prevent.

topics.meaning: An ecosystem is a self-contained unit of living organisms interacting with each other and with their non-living surroundings.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: An ecosystem consists of all the living organisms in an area along with the non-living physical factors of the environment, interacting with each other. The living or biotic components include producers, consumers, and decomposers, while the non-living or abiotic components include factors such as temperature, rainfall, wind, soil, and minerals. Ecosystems can be natural, like forests, ponds, and lakes, or artificial, like crop fields, gardens, and aquariums made by human beings. In an ecosystem, the biotic and abiotic components interact constantly, with energy and nutrients flowing between them. This balance allows the ecosystem to function and sustain the organisms living within it.

topics.meaning: Producers make their own food, consumers depend on others, and decomposers break down dead matter.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The biotic components of an ecosystem are grouped according to how they obtain food. Producers are green plants and certain bacteria that make their own food through photosynthesis using sunlight, and they form the base of the ecosystem. Consumers are organisms that depend on producers or other organisms for their food, and they are further divided into herbivores that eat plants, carnivores that eat other animals, omnivores that eat both, and parasites. Decomposers are micro-organisms such as bacteria and fungi that break down the dead remains of plants and animals into simpler substances. This breakdown returns nutrients to the soil, where they can be used again by producers, completing the cycle of materials in nature.
topics.easyExplanation: Producers make food using sunlight, consumers eat other organisms, and decomposers break down dead matter into nutrients.

topics.meaning: A food chain is a series of organisms feeding one upon another, and a food web is an interconnected network of food chains.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: A food chain shows the series of organisms in an ecosystem through which food and energy pass from one to the next, beginning with the producers. Each step or level in a food chain is called a trophic level: producers form the first trophic level, herbivores the second, small carnivores the third, and larger carnivores the fourth. In a real ecosystem, organisms usually feed on more than one kind of food, so several food chains are interconnected to form a food web. Food chains and food webs show how energy and nutrients move through an ecosystem. The food chain is generally not very long because energy is lost at each trophic level, limiting the number of levels.
topics.easyExplanation: A food chain shows who eats whom in order, while a food web is many food chains linked together.

topics.topic: Flow of Energy in an Ecosystem
topics.meaning: Energy flows through an ecosystem in one direction, decreasing at each trophic level according to the ten percent law.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The flow of energy in an ecosystem begins with the Sun, whose energy is captured by green plants during photosynthesis and converted into chemical energy stored in food. This energy then passes from producers to consumers through the food chain. The flow of energy is unidirectional, meaning it moves only in one direction and does not return to the Sun or back to the previous level. According to the ten percent law, only about ten percent of the energy at one trophic level is passed on to the next level, while the rest is lost mainly as heat used in life processes. Because so much energy is lost at each step, food chains generally have only three or four trophic levels, and the number of organisms decreases at higher levels.
topics.easyExplanation: Energy flows one way through an ecosystem from the Sun, and only about ten percent passes from one level to the next.

topics.meaning: Ecological pyramids show the relationship between trophic levels, and biological magnification is the increase of harmful chemicals along a food chain.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: An ecological pyramid is a graphical representation of the number, biomass, or energy of organisms at each trophic level of a food chain, with producers at the base and top carnivores at the apex. As we move up the trophic levels, the number of organisms and the available energy generally decrease. Biological magnification, also called biomagnification, is the process in which harmful chemicals such as pesticides enter a food chain and accumulate in increasing amounts at each higher trophic level. Since human beings often occupy the top of the food chain, the concentration of these harmful chemicals can become greatest in our bodies. This shows how pollutants introduced into the environment can have serious effects throughout an ecosystem.

topics.meaning: Decomposition is the breakdown of dead matter by decomposers, and proper waste disposal is important to keep the environment clean.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Decomposition is the natural process in which decomposers like bacteria and fungi break down the dead remains of plants and animals into simpler substances. This process returns nutrients to the soil and keeps the environment clean by removing dead matter. Wastes produced by human activities are of two types: biodegradable wastes, which can be broken down by decomposers, such as vegetable peels and paper; and non-biodegradable wastes, which cannot be broken down by natural processes, such as plastics, glass, and metals. Non-biodegradable wastes persist in the environment for a long time and cause pollution. Proper disposal and management of waste, including reducing, reusing, and recycling, are important to protect the environment and human health.

topics.meaning: The ozone layer protects life on Earth by absorbing harmful ultraviolet radiation, but it is being depleted by certain chemicals.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Ozone is a molecule made of three atoms of oxygen and is found high up in the atmosphere, where it forms a layer called the ozone layer. While ozone at ground level is a harmful pollutant, the ozone layer high in the atmosphere is essential because it absorbs most of the harmful ultraviolet radiation coming from the Sun. Ultraviolet radiation is damaging to living organisms and can cause skin cancer and other harmful effects in humans. In recent decades, the amount of ozone in the atmosphere has been dropping sharply because of man-made chemicals called chlorofluorocarbons, or CFCs, which were used in refrigerators and fire extinguishers. To protect the ozone layer, many countries agreed in 1987 to freeze and reduce the production of CFCs.

topics.meaning: Managing garbage involves disposing of and reducing waste responsibly to keep the environment healthy.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The increasing amount of garbage produced by human activities has become a serious problem for the environment. Wastes are broadly biodegradable, which decomposers can break down, and non-biodegradable, which remain in the environment for very long periods. Improvements in our lifestyle have led to greater use of packaging and disposable items, increasing the amount of waste. Methods of waste disposal include burying in landfills, composting of biodegradable waste, recycling of materials like paper, glass, and metals, and proper treatment of sewage. Reducing the use of materials, reusing items where possible, and recycling are important practices that lower the burden of waste. Responsible waste management helps prevent pollution and protects the health of the environment and people.

topics.meaning: Human activities affect the environment in many ways, often disturbing the natural balance of ecosystems.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Human beings depend on the environment for resources, but many human activities disturb the natural balance of ecosystems. The disposal of non-biodegradable wastes such as plastics pollutes the land and water, while the use of harmful chemicals like pesticides leads to biological magnification along food chains. The release of certain gases damages the protective ozone layer, and the cutting of forests reduces the number of producers and disturbs food chains and webs. Disturbing one component of an ecosystem can affect many others, because all the organisms and physical factors are linked. Therefore, it is important for human beings to use resources wisely, manage waste properly, and protect ecosystems so that the environment remains healthy for all living organisms.
